task: Force Day 1 install UX to ask AI tool choice first and refresh stale Mac mini sessions

governance_bootstrap.summary: Completion requires evidence-backed reporting, entrypoint verification, and completion evidence guard for code, UI, deploy, and customer-facing answer changes.
Mac Mini production changes require source-of-truth deploy flow, real user entrypoint verification, and completion evidence guard before reporting complete.

changes: Day 1 install guide now always starts with choosing or confirming an AI tool, even when the saved preferred tool is Claude.
Day 1 install guide always exposes ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Genspark, and Grok options to the frontend.
TrainingScreen now renders the AI tool options as visible chips and highlights the currently selected tool.

Legacy Day 1 migration now refreshes completed and uncompleted saved sessions when old Claude-only install copy or old practice_lab_version is present, while preserving completion state, saved result, notes, checked steps, questions, answers, and feedback.
Added regression coverage for completed stale Day 1 sessions so old copy is not kept after refresh.

verification.name: targeted Day 1 tool choice tests
verification.command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_edu_vp_training_flow.py -k 'day1_install_guide or beginner_friendly_source_language or refresh_adds_guided_practice_lab or refresh_replaces_legacy_day1_rag_copy or refresh_replaces_completed_legacy_day1_install_copy'

verification.name: full edu VP training flow tests
edu app production build
verification.result: passed: tsc and vite built dist/assets/index-D5_gMms7.js

residual_risk: A browser holding an older JS asset may need a hard refresh, but the deployed HTML should point to the new hashed bundle after Mac mini rebuild.
